title: IRGC announces new maritime control zone in Strait of Hormuz
snippet: The IRGC declared a new 'smart control' maritime zone in the Strait of Hormuz. Southern boundary: Mount Mobarak (Iran) to south of Fujairah (UAE). Western boundary: end of Qeshm Island (Iran) to Umm Al Quwain (UAE). The IRGC spokesperson warned vessels violating transit protocols would be 'forcefully stopped.' Ship-tracking data showed only 9 vessels moved through the passage in the previous 24 hours. Ali Abdollahi (Khatam al-Anbia Central HQ Commander) warned any foreign military force approaching Hormuz will come under attack. Called Project Freedom and the US naval blockade acts of 'piracy' and a violation of the April 8 ceasefire.
tag_reason: IRGC formally declares maritime control zone with specific coordinates — escalation from de facto to de jure control of Hormuz.
